nodes.label: User Symptom Tracking
nodes.description: Users can log their asthma severity and specific symptoms.

nodes.label: Predictive Flare-Up Insights
nodes.description: The system should predict when a user's asthma might worsen based on various data points.

nodes.label: Proactive AI-Powered Advice
nodes.description: An LLM-backed advisor provides proactive advice during high-risk periods.

nodes.label: Persistent Data Storage
nodes.description: All user-generated data, including symptoms and logs, must be saved in a persistent database.

nodes.label: One-Way Calendar Push
nodes.description: Enable a one-way push of high-risk alerts from the platform to a user's external calendar (e.g., Google Calendar).

nodes.label: Unattended Operation with Error Recovery
nodes.description: The system must run unattended with full error recovery, requiring no human intervention once started.

nodes.label: Initial Mock Data
nodes.description: The system must include mock symptom data for initial model training and test user data.

nodes.label: Clear Medical Disclaimer
nodes.description: The application must display a clear, concise disclaimer stating that it is not a substitute for professional medical advice and that users should consult a doctor.

nodes.label: PHI/HIPAA Compliance
nodes.description: The system must be designed to be compliant with regulations for handling Personal Health Information (e.g., HIPAA), as it is intended for both personal and commercial use.

nodes.label: Availability of External APIs
nodes.description: Assumes that external APIs for weather, air quality, and the LLM are consistently available and reliable.

nodes.label: Privacy and Health Data Compliance
nodes.description: Handling sensitive personal health information (PHI) for a commercial product introduces significant legal and ethical risks (e.g., HIPAA). A data breach could have severe consequences.

nodes.label: Medical Advice Liability
nodes.description: Providing incorrect or misinterpreted medical advice could lead to adverse health outcomes for users and legal liability for the platform.

nodes.label: Free Tier API Limitations
nodes.description: Free APIs for weather and air quality often have strict rate limits, lower data accuracy, and no guarantee of service, which could degrade the predictive model's quality.

nodes.label: Use React (with Vite) for Frontend
nodes.description: The web application's user interface will be a single-page application (SPA) built using the React library and Vite for the build tooling.
nodes.rationale: React is a widely adopted, flexible library for building user interfaces. Using it as a SPA separates concerns cleanly from the backend API. Vite provides a fast and modern development experience.

nodes.label: Use FastAPI for Backend
nodes.description: The backend API will be developed using FastAPI, a modern Python web framework.
nodes.rationale: FastAPI is chosen for its high performance, asynchronous capabilities, and strong integration with Python's data science and machine learning libraries (like Scikit-learn, Pandas), which are essential for the predictive model.

nodes.label: Use PostgreSQL for Database
nodes.description: PostgreSQL will be the primary database for storing all application data, including user info and symptom logs.
nodes.rationale: PostgreSQL is a robust, open-source, and highly scalable relational database with strong security features suitable for handling sensitive PHI and meeting compliance requirements for a commercial application.

nodes.label: Use Free Tier External APIs
nodes.description: The system will use free or free-tier APIs for external data, such as OpenWeatherMap for weather and OpenAQ for air quality.
nodes.rationale: This approach minimizes initial operational costs, which is suitable for the early stages of a project. The system should be designed to easily swap providers if needed.

nodes.label: Use OAuth 2.0 and JWT for Auth
nodes.description: API authentication will be handled using the OAuth 2.0 protocol, with JWTs issued to clients to authorize access to protected endpoints.
nodes.rationale: This is a secure, industry-standard approach for web and mobile applications that is well-supported and essential for protecting sensitive user data (PHI).

nodes.label: Use Celery and Redis for Task Scheduling
nodes.description: Background tasks (data ingestion, model training) will be managed and executed by Celery workers, using Redis as the message broker.
nodes.rationale: Celery is a robust and scalable distributed task queue for Python, ideal for handling the required unattended operations. Redis is a fast, reliable, and commonly used broker for Celery.

nodes.label: Use OpenAI API for AI Advisor
nodes.description: The AI advisor character will be powered by an OpenAI GPT model via their API.
nodes.rationale: OpenAI provides powerful, general-purpose language models that are well-suited for generating conversational and context-aware advice. Their APIs are mature and well-documented.

nodes.label: Use Docker for Containerization
nodes.description: All services (frontend, backend, workers, database) will be containerized using Docker.
nodes.rationale: Docker provides a consistent, isolated, and reproducible environment for development, testing, and production, simplifying deployment and scaling.

nodes.label: Use Sentry for Error Monitoring
nodes.description: The application will be integrated with Sentry to provide real-time error tracking and reporting for both the frontend and backend.
nodes.rationale: Sentry offers detailed error reports, alerts, and performance monitoring, which are critical for maintaining a reliable, unattended system.

nodes.label: React Frontend Application
nodes.description: A Single-Page Application (SPA) that provides the user interface for symptom logging, data visualization, and viewing AI advisor messages.

nodes.label: FastAPI Backend Server
nodes.description: The central API server that handles business logic, user authentication, data persistence, and communication with external services.

nodes.label: Prediction Service
nodes.description: A service within the backend responsible for training and running the predictive model. It fetches user history, weather, and air quality data to generate flare-up risk scores.

nodes.label: AI Advisor Service
nodes.description: A service that connects to an external LLM API. It uses the prediction service's output to generate and deliver proactive, personalized advice to the user.

nodes.label: Data Ingestion Service
nodes.description: A background service responsible for periodically fetching data from external weather and air quality APIs and storing it in the database.

nodes.label: Calendar Integration Service
nodes.description: A service that handles OAuth and API interactions with external calendar providers (e.g., Google Calendar) to push high-risk event notifications.

nodes.label: Authentication Service
nodes.description: A service within the backend that manages user registration, login, and the issuance/validation of JWTs based on the OAuth 2.0 flow.

nodes.label: Celery Task Queue
nodes.description: The distributed task queue system that schedules and executes background jobs like data ingestion and model prediction runs.

nodes.label: Redis Message Broker
nodes.description: The message broker that facilitates communication between the main backend application and the Celery workers.

nodes.label: Main REST API
nodes.description: The primary RESTful interface exposed by the backend server for the frontend application to consume. It includes endpoints for authentication, symptom logging, and retrieving predictions.

nodes.label: Unit Tests
nodes.description: Unit tests will be written for individual functions and components in isolation to verify their correctness.

nodes.label: Integration Tests
nodes.description: Integration tests will verify that different components of the system work together as expected (e.g., API endpoint calls the correct service).

nodes.label: End-to-End Tests
nodes.description: E2E tests will simulate real user workflows from the browser to ensure the entire system functions correctly.
